rows.source_ref: muhiuddin (handle), "Best Trading System (Only Momentum)" [MOMENTUM PRICE SYSTEM], ForexFactory thread 423512, forums.forexfactory.com, n.d. — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/423512/best-trading-system-only-momentum
rows.concept: Trade regular price/Momentum(28) divergence: enter on the close after momentum breaks its intra-divergence extreme (point F), betting the divergence resolves into a reversal.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic entry/exit/TP once divergence is fixed, pure-MQL5, stops + defined DD (FTMO/DXZ-compatible); but divergence-line identification is subjective and needs a mechanized swing/pivot heuristic.
rows.notes: Rules: Momentum(28,close) on H4-D1. Bearish setup=price HH vs momentum LH; mark highest price as C, lowest momentum as F; short 2 lots on bar-close when F is penetrated. Bullish=mirror. SL at price C. TP1=|C-entry| (exit 1 lot, SL->BE), TP2=2xTP1 (trail 50% of profit each new candle), TP3=4xTP1 (trail 75%). Divergence swing lookback 8-28 bars. Author claims FX majors+crosses (incl JPY crosses: CADJPY/GBPJPY) + crude oil (XTIUSD), untested gold/silver; anecdotal forward-test only, no backtest, self-reported ~3.5-10%/mo with 32% DD, and multiple posters note different brokers show different momentum values -> divergence non-reproducible. Variant: pampaq applied same divergence idea to CCI14 on M15/M60 (same core, not a separate row). homealbum's EMA(21,8)+Bollinger 'price action' comment and KnSquare's Donchian breakout aside are too vague to be candidates. If built, the divergence detector (pivot-based regular-divergence, F=momentum extreme break confirmation) is the load-bearing mechanization risk.

notes: single thread = one implementable strategy (momentum-divergence swing); remaining posts are vague commentary or attached indicators, not distinct candidates
